back: A model trained on massive text that generates answers by predicting the next token, over and over.
A chunk of text (about 3/4 of a word) that the model reads and writes. Text is chopped into tokens and handled as numbers.
The maximum amount of text (in tokens) a model can consider at once - prompt plus answer. Like a desk of fixed size.
The billions of adjustable numbers inside a model where its learned knowledge lives. More parameters = a bigger model.
The one-time learning phase: guess the next token on mountains of text, adjust when wrong, repeat billions of times.
Using the trained model - what happens every time you send a prompt and it generates tokens.
A dial controlling randomness in token choice. Low = safe and consistent; high = creative but erratic.
When a model confidently produces plausible-sounding but false information. Probability, not truth - it guesses instead of going silent.
An LLM that generates step-by-step 'thinking' tokens before its final answer. Slower, but far better at hard problems.
Extra training of a finished model on a focused dataset to make it a specialist - like a doctor doing a specialization.
